pr.title: Split out known ft files from ft_08A4
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Mechanically split a very large fighter assembly bucket, asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, into 17 smaller known ft assembly files and added those objects to obj_files.mk immediately after ft_08A4.s.o. The PR did not decompile code to C or add review discussion; its value was repo organization: reducing the monolithic ft_08A4.s by roughly 39.5k deleted lines while preserving build order through explicit TEXT_O_FILES entries.
what_changed: Removed the bulk of known fighter assembly regions from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s: the changed-file counts show 39,509 deleted lines and 417 added lines in that original bucket.

Created separate assembly files under asm/melee/ft for known or semi-known ft components: ftmaterial.s, ftcolanim.s, ftdevice.s, ftafterimage.s, ftmetal.s, ftswing.s, ftstarrodswing.s, ftlipstickswing.s, ftattacks4combo.s, ftchangeparam.s, plus address-range files ft_0C08.s, ft_0C31.s, ft_0C88.s, ft_0CD1.s, ft_0CDD.s, ft_0CEE.s, and ft_0D14.s.
Updated obj_files.mk by adding 17 new asm object entries after $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o in TEXT_O_FILES.

The diff excerpt shows code beginning around ftCo_800BF228 in ft_08A4.s, with large blocks such as .L_800BF260, .L_800BF2B8, .L_800BF534, and .L_800BF6BC removed from the monolithic file and relocated elsewhere.
smart_moves: Kept the change mechanical and organizational: no evidence of semantic rewrites, making the huge line churn safer to review as a file-boundary split.

Preserved linker/build ordering explicitly in obj_files.mk by placing the new split assembly objects immediately after ft_08A4.s.o, which is important for assembly buckets where object order can affect matching addresses and symbol layout.
Used descriptive file names where subsystem identity was known, such as ftmaterial, ftcolanim, ftafterimage, ftmetal, ftswing, ftstarrodswing, ftlipstickswing, ftattacks4combo, and ftchangeparam.

Kept unknown or less confidently named regions as address-range-style files like ft_0C08.s, ft_0C31.s, ft_0C88.s, ft_0CD1.s, ft_0CDD.s, ft_0CEE.s, and ft_0D14.s rather than inventing unsupported subsystem names.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic assembly bucket contains recognizable subsystems, split known regions into named files before or alongside C decompilation work; this improves searchability and future ownership without requiring a matching C rewrite.
For large asm splits, build-script ordering is part of the correctness story. Add each new .s.o to TEXT_O_FILES in the same relative location as the original bucket content.
Prefer descriptive subsystem names only when the identity is already known; otherwise retain address-range filenames to avoid misleading future decompilers.
A large negative diff in the original asm file paired with equivalent additions in new asm files should be treated as a relocation/refactor, not necessarily progress in C matching.
naming_conventions: Known fighter subsystem assembly files use lowercase ft-prefixed names, e.g. ftmaterial.s, ftcolanim.s, ftafterimage.s, ftmetal.s, ftswing.s, ftchangeparam.s.
Item/swing-related fighter helper files use specific compound lowercase names, e.g. ftstarrodswing.s, ftlipstickswing.s, ftattacks4combo.s.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic split files retain address-range names in the existing style: ft_0C08.s, ft_0C31.s, ft_0C88.s, ft_0CD1.s, ft_0CDD.s, ft_0CEE.s, ft_0D14.s.
The build entries mirror source paths as $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/<file>.s.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical assembly extraction from a monolithic bucket into multiple .s translation units.
Preserve assembly labels and address-derived local labels such as .L_800BF260, .L_800BF2B8, .L_800BF534, and .L_800BF6BC when moving code.
Preserve section data and embedded strings during splits; the diff excerpt shows .section .sdata, .asciz strings, .balign directives, and jump-table-style .4byte entries in the moved material.
Maintain object-file order in obj_files.mk to avoid unintended text/data layout changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No human PR body text was provided, so inferred intent is limited to the title and diff.
searchable_terms: Split out known ft files from ft_08A4
follow_up_queries: Which later PRs decompile ftmaterial.s or ftcolanim.s from asm to C?
Which files split from ft_08A4.s still remain as address-range assembly buckets?
Did later changes rename ft_0C08.s, ft_0C31.s, ft_0C88.s, ft_0CD1.s, ft_0CDD.s, ft_0CEE.s, or ft_0D14.s to semantic subsystem names?
Are obj_files.mk ordering constraints documented for assembly splits in asm/melee/ft?
